reforms.description: The National Water Initiative (NWI) was agreed upon to enhance water reform in Australia, focusing on improving the security of water access entitlements and encouraging the expansion of water markets and trading.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative aims to improve water access and market efficiency, which can enhance productivity in various sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The NWI has been fully implemented as part of Australia's water reform agenda.
reforms.importance_rationale: The NWI represents a significant structural change in water management policy.
reforms.source_quote: The NWI aims to improve the security of water access entitlements, ensure ecosystems’ health and encourage the expansion of water markets and trading.

reforms.description: In February 2006, COAG agreed to collaborate on the implementation of a new National Reform Agenda that includes a human capital agenda focusing on health, education, and training, alongside traditional areas of competition and regulatory reform.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The focus on health, education, and training is expected to enhance the skills and capabilities of the workforce, driving innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The agenda has been actively pursued and implemented by COAG since its agreement.
reforms.importance_rationale: The National Reform Agenda represents a comprehensive approach to reforming human capital development in Australia.
reforms.source_quote: In February 2006, COAG agreed to collaborate on the implementation of a new National Reform Agenda to enhance productivity and labour force participation.
